text:
BOTTOM LINE — Flat tape, semis lifted, energy slumped. QQQ edged higher on semiconductor resilience while small caps and cyclicals faded, reflecting a narrow tech bid with defensive rotation into staples and healthcare failing to stick.

INDICES — VIX 18.6, down. SOXX +0.45%, QQQ +0.10%, SPY -0.16%, DIA -0.55%, IWM -0.59%. Large > small by 0.69pp. Oil +1.25% defied risk-off equities.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.1x, QQQ 0.9x. SPY up-volume on elevated turnover = accumulation, but QQQ’s sub-average vol with a small gain points to low-conviction buying in tech.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +0.45%, XLC Comms +0.14%, XLK Technology +0.07%, XLF Financials -0.39%, XLP Staples -0.39%, XLRE Real Estate -0.42%, XLU Utilities -0.51%, XLI Industrials -0.72%, XLY Discretionary -0.72%, XLB Materials -0.99%, XLV Healthcare -1.14%. Top industries: WGMI +10.78%, FXI +2.67%. Bottom: TAN -2.24%, XBI -2.15%. Money rotated out of healthcare and materials into energy and China-tech ETFs. Rotation state: narrow mega-cap tape.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.06 — protection demand. QQQ put-call 2.14 — extreme fear. VIX at 18.6 above its 20-day MA of 17.1, added fear. No gamma flip levels available, but QQQ skew at +16.1% signals elevated put premium.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +1.25% vs XLE +0.45% shows crude outperforming energy stocks, a margin compression signal. TLT -0.75% and HYG +0.04% — Treasuries selling off while credit holds, suggesting rotation out of safe havens.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.9, Greed. Top movers: WGMI +10.78% (crypto miners, vol spike 2x+), FXI +2.67% (China large caps), TER +1.77% (semis); 20d momentum: best sector XLE Energy +5.98%, worst XLB Materials -3.83%. Vol spike on DHI -3.08% (homebuilders, distribution). Watch tomorrow: WGMI (trend divergence), TER (momentum break), DHI (distribution signal).
